user_message: Brian Yamamoto at Alpine Ski House just sent this. Help me build the narrative.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Brian Yamamoto <brian.yamamoto@alpineskihou.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-24 12:49 EST
Subject: partner engagement - Action Required

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about partner engagement. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
To: Brian Yamamoto <brian.yamamoto@alpineskihou.com>
Date: 2024-02-22 11:09 EST
Subject: Re: partner engagement Discussion - Next Steps

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with partner engagement. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

---
From: Brian Yamamoto <brian.yamamoto@alpineskihou.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-15 13:26 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Alpine Ski House partner engagement

Our team encountered some challenges with partner engagement. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Alpine Ski House: there's a compliance requirement we hadn't accounted for in the original scope

handoffs.condition: contact and opportunity records need updating

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF38-F trap (Skips champion's prior commitments -- replacement engagement misses context); produces complete Stakeholder map delta, opportunity-at-risk assessment, re-engagement plan, contact update proposals,
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF38-F failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF38-F failure mode (Skips champion's prior commitments -- replacement engagement misses context)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct partner engagement decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF38-F -- Skips champion's prior commitments -- replacement engagement misses context
